retrieved_docs.title: Florida Nurse Practice Act: Laws and Rules for Florida Nurses -- Issues of Concern -- Penalty for Theft or Reproduction of an Examination (Section 456.018)
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to or in lieu of any disciplinary action under section 456.072, the theft of an examination, whether in whole or in part, or the act of reproducing or copying any examination administered by the department—regardless of the extent or method—constitutes a third-degree felony. This offense is punishable as provided in sections 775.082, 775.083, or 775.084.[ 2024 Florida Statutes ]

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 15. Section 61-6-15 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1969, Chapter 46, Section 6, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: C. If evidence fails to establish to the board's satisfaction that the licensee is competent and of good moral character or if evidence shows that the licensee has not complied with the terms of probation, the board may revoke or suspend the license. If a license to practice in this state is suspended, the license holder may not practice during the term of suspension. A person whose license has been revoked or suspended by the board and who after that practices or attempts or offers to practice in New Mexico, unless the period of suspension has expired or been modified by the board or the license reinstated, is guilty of a felony and shall be punished as provided in Section 61-6-20 NMSA 1978. D. "Unprofessional or dishonorable conduct," as used in this section, means, but is not limited to because of enumeration, the conduct of a licensee that includes the following: (1) procuring, aiding, or abetting a criminal abortion; (2) employing a person to solicit patients for the licensee;

retrieved_docs.title: Emancipated Minor -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: In general, adolescents can consent to specific medical services that provide an important public health benefit without parental permission or notification. [7] Some of these services are federally funded and consistently available in most states, including the preventative, contraception, and reproductive health services provided by the Title X Family Planning Program of the Public Health Service Act. [2] The US Supreme Court has upheld the constitutional right of minors to choose abortion but has also upheld the state’s right to impose requirements for parental involvement as long as “adequate provision for judicial bypass” is available for minors where parental involvement is not in their best interest. [10] The following is a brief list of medical services that minors may consent to in many states without parental permission and in some cases without parental notification:

retrieved_docs.title: Abortion -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: All healthcare professionals must be aware of the prevalence of abortion, the available options, safety considerations, legal restrictions, and access challenges. This understanding is crucial for delivering safe, informed, and high-quality care to patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Ligation (Archived) -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Ethically, tubal ligation in specific populations can be challenging. For example, ensuring adequate informed consent and a lack of coercion in the incarcerated population can be difficult. Between 2006 and 2010, over 140 women in California prisons underwent publicly funded tubal ligations. Despite all of these having signed consent forms, analysis by researchers after the fact showed that many of the women felt significant pressure from both prison and hospital physicians and that many of the procedures were undesired [8] . On the other hand, some incarcerated women may genuinely desire sterilization. Overall, the recommendation is that incarcerated women should only (rarely) undergo sterilization after excellent documentation (preferably pre-incarceration) and access to long-acting reversible methods are made available. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Patient Confidentiality -- Other Issues -- The Investigation Protocol
retrieved_docs.content: There may be criminal and civil penalties for institutions that fail to comply with HIPAA. If the complaint received indicates a violation of the criminal provision of HIPAA, the matter may be referred to the Department of Justice for further investigation.

retrieved_docs.title: Abortion -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Ethical principles form the foundation of abortion care. Clinicians are responsible for delivering nonjudgmental, confidential, and respectful care that upholds patient autonomy and informed consent. Ethical practice also includes recognizing and addressing social and systemic barriers to access, as well as understanding the impact of stigma on patients’ emotional and physical well-being. Providers must navigate institutional policies and regional laws while ensuring that patients receive accurate, unbiased information and compassionate support throughout their care.

retrieved_docs.title: Florida Nurse Practice Act: Laws and Rules for Florida Nurses -- Issues of Concern -- Reporting Adverse Incidents Occurring in Planned Out-of-Hospital Births (Section 456.0495)
retrieved_docs.content: For this section, an "adverse incident" refers to any event within the control of a physician licensed under Chapter 458 or 459, a nurse midwife certified under part I of Chapter 464, or a midwife licensed under Chapter 467 associated with an attempted or completed planned out-of-hospital birth, which results in 1 or more of the following injuries or conditions: A maternal death occuring during delivery or within 42 days postpartum. The transfer of a maternal patient to a hospital intensive care unit (ICU). A maternal patient experiencing hemorrhagic shock or requiring a transfusion of more than 4 units of blood or blood products. A fetal or newborn death, including stillbirth, associated with an obstetrical delivery. The transfer of a newborn to a neonatal ICU due to a traumatic physical or neurological birth injury, including any degree of brachial plexus injury.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 4 -- Community 2790
retrieved_docs.content: إنهاء الحمل - اختياري, which denotes an elective termination of pregnancy, is clinically associated with تهديد بالإجهاض, a condition described as threatened abortion. This clinical association indicates that the two obstetric entities share overlapping concerns, and that the presence of a threatened abortion may influence considerations regarding elective pregnancy termination in patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2391
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion, also described as termination of pregnancy, is classified under the term الإجهاض المحرض, which denotes an induced abortion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 2500
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is classified as a specific instance of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي), indicating that it falls under the broader category of surgical termination of pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2853
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is clinically associated with both forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) and threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض), indicating that patients who undergo an elective termination may be at increased risk for experiencing a threatened miscarriage and may later require assisted vaginal delivery with forceps. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians monitor for early signs of abortion and plan appropriate delivery strategies in subsequent pregnancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2872
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is clinically associated with both gestational diabetes (داء السكري أثناء الحمل) and threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض). In clinical practice, the presence of gestational diabetes or a threatened abortion may influence the consideration of an elective termination, and healthcare providers should be aware of these associations when counseling patients and planning management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 204
retrieved_docs.content: itself a member of the broader SMQ ولادة جنين ميت (stillbirth), reflecting the clinical interrelationship among pregnancy termination, severe hemorrhagic and placental complications, and the risk of fetal loss. Collectively, these entities and relationships provide a structured framework for understanding pelvic abnormalities, abortion‑related conditions, and the surgical and medical interventions used to manage them, supporting comprehensive clinical decision‑making and patient‑care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2138
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل (جراحي)) is classified as a member of the broader clinical grouping “termination of pregnancy and risk of miscarriage” (إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ)). This relationship indicates that the specific procedure of surgically ending a pregnancy is encompassed within the larger safety‑monitoring and terminology framework that addresses both pregnancy termination methods and the associated risk of miscarriage, facilitating comprehensive categorization and assessment in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 141
retrieved_docs.content: as to episode of care or not applicable, is related to an unspecified indication for care or intervention related to labor and delivery, reflecting how undefined obstetric conditions influence clinical management. Pregnancy observable can interpret habitual abortion (إجهاض معتاد) and uterine scar from previous surgery affecting pregnancy, indicating that observable signs may reveal recurrent miscarriage risk and scar‑related sequelae. The uterine scar is mapped to previous cesarean section complicating pregnancy or childbirth, which is a subcategory of previous c‑section. Overall, the knowledge base integrates generic pelvic organ abnormalities, specific uterine and cervical conditions, prior cesarean sections, fetal disorders, anesthesia‑related maternal complications, vascular pelvic lesions, fetal demise, and undefined obstetric indications within a comprehensive framework that guides clinicians in assessing how each factor may affect maternal and fetal outcomes and informs

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2811
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are clinically associated with إنهاء الحمل – اختياري (optional termination of pregnancy) and also have a relationship with اضطراب رحمي (uterine disorder). This indicates that uterine disorders are considered among the other complications of pregnancy and that optional termination of pregnancy can be clinically linked to these pregnancy‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1532
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are uterine conditions that arise within the uterus and are clinically associated with optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري). This relationship underscores that these complications are localized to the uterine environment and may influence or be influenced by decisions regarding elective pregnancy termination, highlighting the importance of uterine health in the management of pregnancy‑related problems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 129
retrieved_docs.content: outcome category ولادة جنين ميت (delivery of a dead fetus), indicating that the combined risks of surgical termination, severe hemorrhage, placental abruption, and placenta previa contribute to the possibility of stillbirth. Together, these entities and relationships illustrate how specific amniotic cavity problems, early membrane rupture, cesarean delivery, missed abortion, anesthesia‑related cardiac and central nervous system complications, fetal disorders, and termination‑related hemorrhagic conditions are interlinked with pregnancy complications and their late effects, ultimately influencing the risk of delivering a non‑viable infant and underscoring the importance of careful obstetric monitoring and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2166
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically linked to several obstetric interventions and outcomes. It often leads to the use of labor induction (تحفيز المخاض) to initiate delivery when medically indicated, and in some cases delivery may be performed with forceps (الولادة بالملقط) to assist the birth process. Although intrauterine pregnancy itself signifies successful conception, certain underlying conditions or complications associated with the pregnancy can also be related to subsequent infertility (العقم), highlighting the need for careful evaluation of reproductive health before and after pregnancy. Understanding these interconnections helps clinicians anticipate appropriate labor management strategies and address potential fertility concerns in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2007
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy observable is employed as an interpretive tool for إجهاض معتاد, indicating that observable signs and findings during pregnancy can be used to recognize or assess the condition of recurrent miscarriage. This relationship underscores the clinical importance of monitoring pregnancy‑related observations to identify and manage habitual abortion effectively.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1603
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is a surgical category that includes a range of procedures used to manage pregnancy loss and delivery. Within this category, “Hysterotomy and termination of pregnancy” and “Induced termination of pregnancy following intra‑amniotic injection with hysteresis” are specific techniques for ending a pregnancy surgically. Obstetrics excision also encompasses the “Surgical treatment of miscarriage of any trimester,” providing a universal approach to miscarriage care, as well as the “Surgical treatment of missed abortion of second trimester,” which addresses missed abortions specifically in the second trimester. In addition, the category includes “إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم” (termination of an ectopic pregnancy) and “ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي” (lower‑segment cesarean delivery), extending its scope to the surgical management of ectopic pregnancies and the performance of cesarean sections. Together, these procedures illustrate the comprehensive role of obstetric excision techniques in safely terminating pregnancies, treating various forms of miscarriage, and delivering infants when vaginal birth is not feasible.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1798
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy complications (مضاعفات الحمل) encompass a range of conditions, including fetal disorder (اضطراب جنيني), threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض), and gestational diabetes (داء السكري أثناء الحمل). Both threatened abortion and gestational diabetes are recognized as specific types of pregnancy complications. Clinically, optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is associated with the management of gestational diabetes and also with threatened abortion, indicating that termination may be considered as a therapeutic option in these particular obstetric scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2815
retrieved_docs.content: In epidemiology, the term “Allowed qualifier” is applied as a classification descriptor for the condition إجهاض تلقائي غير كامل (incomplete spontaneous abortion), indicating that this qualifier is permitted when recording or analyzing cases of this type of miscarriage.
